steps.title: Parallel lines have equal slopes — read slope directly from standard form
steps.content:
For $ax + by = c$ the slope is $-a/b$, no algebra needed.
Line $j$: $5x + 8y = 32$ $\Rightarrow$ slope $= -5/8$.
Line $k$: $20x + ry = 20$ $\Rightarrow$ slope $= -20/r$.
Set equal: $-5/8 = -20/r$ $\Rightarrow$ $5r = 160$ $\Rightarrow$ $r = 32$.

steps.title: Plug the y-intercept point into line k
steps.content:
Line $k$ is $20x + 32y = 20$. The point $(0, b)$ lies on it, so substitute $x = 0$:
$$32b = 20 \implies b = \frac{5}{8}$$
The answer is $\boxed{0.625}$ (or $5/8$).
